'''Raze''' was the head of a multi-[[Species/Legends|species]] information brokering organization.

==Biography==
[[File:Razebody.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Raze's cybernetic body.]]

Intelligent even for his [[Species/Legends|species]], he was capable of sorting data at superhuman speeds; Raze was an intimidating figure with his multi-segmented, centipedal [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic body]], which included a pair of heavy [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] on a [[Scorpion/Legends|scorpion]]-like tail. For unknown reasons, he had an implant in his skull and down his spine that allowed his [[Brain/Legends|brain]] to be seen. It may have had to do with his mental control of the cybernetic portion of his body.

Based out of the [[Per Lupelo]] [[Star system/Legends|system]], his organization relied on taps into various lines of communication along with an army of [[Spy/Legends|spies]] and [[Information broker/Legends|information gatherers]] throughout the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Raze's top [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]] within the organization were [[Wyl Tarson]], [[Drybal]], a [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] and a [[Ubese/Legends|Ubese]].

When Raze attempted to hijack an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] cargo convoy, they were beaten to the punch by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], acting on information leaked by double agent Tarson. Later, when Tarson attempted to intercept a message in the system for [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] from [[Janek Sunber/Legends|Janek Sunber]], he was discovered by Raze, who implanted a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]] and [[Comlink/Legends|comm]] unit in his skull and sent him on a [[Mission to Ahakista|mission]] to [[Ahakista/Legends|Ahakista]].

Tarson set out to organize a team to accomplish the mission, which was to steal data from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] database. When in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Ahakista, fighters intercepted Tarson's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and demanded the access codes. Raze held off to the last second on giving the code to Tarson in order to prove he was still in control of the situation.

Once on the ground, Tarson became involved in Imperial and rebel intrigue and threatened to stop the mission. [[Laynara]], Tarson's lover, pleaded for his life when Raze activated the bomb in Tarson's head. Suddenly, the link was cut. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] had deactivated the bomb in order to convince Tarson to let him know what was going on. The mission ended with the resistance destroying [[Pyramid One]] and the database.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion: The Ahakista Gambit">''[[Star Wars: Rebellion: The Ahakista Gambit]]''</ref>

Tarson betrayed both Raze and the Rebel Alliance when he told Vader all he knew to save Laynara. The Imperials came to Per Lupelo to [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombard]] Raze's base in retaliation for the earlier loss of their cargo ships. Raze managed to escape in time.

Even with the loss of his power base, he still commanded respect and considerable resources. When his friend [[King/Legends|King]] [[Arturo]] contacted him to rescue his kidnapped daughter, [[Mi]], Raze saw to it personally that bounty hunters were dispatched to rescue the lost princess.<ref name="Star Wars Adventures: Princess Leia and the Royal Ransom">''[[Star Wars Adventures: Princess Leia and the Royal Ransom]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Raze was a greedy individual who ran a vast criminal empire on the planet Per Lupelo. He valued loyalty from his associates, going so far as to send them on [[Death/Legends|suicide]] missions in response to a betrayal. However he also was quick to pay back loyalty with special favors, evidenced by his assistance in rescuing [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] Mi.
